title: Hemigraphis crispa
intro: Hemigraphis crispa, commonly known as silver fall or Thai signal plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Hemigraphis crispa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Hemigraphis crispa include tea, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including coughs, colds, and skin infections.
